summary: Task 5 (AndroidManifest.xml new <activity>, SanctumApplication handler install, SanctumApplicationTest Robolectric reflection) introduces no security findings. A01 access control: CrashReportActivity is declared android:exported="false" with no <intent-filter>, so no implicit export surface; android:taskAffinity="" isolates the :crash task from the main app's task (prevents task-hijack / task-reparenting on same-UID re-association); android:excludeFromRecents="true" keeps the crash surface out of Recents thumbnails. No external app can launch the activity without INTERACT_ACROSS_USERS_FULL (system-only), which cannot be granted to third parties, so the minSdk=31 exported-default still holds under explicit declaration. The launch path from CrashHandler already uses explicit setClassName(packageName, FQN) (audited in task-2/security-auditor-1.json). A05 misconfiguration: android:process=":crash" is a private per-package process; no android:permission is required because exported=false, no android:directBootAware/showWhenLocked/turnScreenOn flags are set (so the crash surface does not bypass the keyguard and cannot leak stacktraces on the lockscreen), no android:launchMode override, no configChanges override. Activity inherits android:allowBackup="false" / usesCleartextTraffic="false" from the <application> element. Theme Theme.Sanctum is the existing app theme — no new theme surface introduced. A08 integrity and re-entry prevention (Decision 4): SanctumApplication.onCreate installs the handler inside `if (getProcessName() == packageName) { installCrashHandler() }` BEFORE the DefaultDownloadRepository.mainActivityFqn assignment, so a ClassLoader/OOM failure on that line is caught by CrashHandler — matches task-5 Acceptance Criteria and tech-spec Decision 4 verbatim. The guard is null-safe: getProcessName() returning null on a hypothetical older runtime would evaluate `null == packageName` → false → no install, no NPE. applicationContext (not `this`) is passed into CrashHandler so the JVM-global UncaughtExceptionHandler does not pin a transient Application reference. Killer.Default is a stable companion object; no construction path that could fail silently. No try/catch around installCrashHandler() — correct per task spec; a throw from the CrashHandler constructor (e.g. OOM) would propagate to the system's default handler and kill the app immediately, which is the desired fail-loud behavior (not a silent A09 swallow). No deserialization, no reflection in production code, no dynamic class loading, no user input flowing into the install path. A09 logging: the handler-install path is non-throwing in steady state and does not itself log; the CrashHandler's own inner try/catch + Log.e breadcrumb path (Task 2 scope) is unaffected. Test file SanctumApplicationTest: test-scope only (src/test), not packaged into the APK. Reflection targets android.app.ActivityThread.mBoundApplication.processName — Robolectric's own shadow field, not a real Android binder/IPC surface; no setAccessible on SecurityManager / ClassLoader / Unsafe. @Before snapshots and @After restores BOTH the global UncaughtExceptionHandler AND the bound process name, so the reflection writes do not leak across sibling test classes in the same Robolectric JVM (mirrors ErrorLogTest's symmetric-cleanup pattern). SentinelHandler is a test-local object with a no-op uncaughtException — no state exposure even if leaked. No credentials, no network I/O, no file I/O outside Robolectric's sandbox. All mandatory checks pass: no hardcoded secrets (Manifest contains only literal string attributes; SanctumApplication.kt contains no API keys / tokens / credentials), no injection surface (no shell exec, no SQL, no dynamic class loading from user input), no SSRF (no URL construction), no CORS / CSP relevance (not a web app), no dependency churn (Acceptance Criteria confirms no new gradle deps). Decisions 3 and 4 honored at the file level.
